rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign non-specific low back pain is normally T1 self-care, but this patient is floor-bound, living alone and unable to transfer, so prompt review/assistance is appropriate (T2) alongside analgesia and reassurance. Cauda equina carries a hard 000/ED (T5) safety-net; fracture/infection/malignancy red flags route to urgent assessment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of bladder or bowel control, inability to pass urine despite feeling full, numbness around the groin/buttocks/inner thighs, or progressive/bilateral leg weakness or numbness
escalation_edges.rationale: Cauda equina syndrome — a surgical emergency; delay causes permanent damage.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you lose control of your bladder or bowels, can't pass urine, get numbness around your groin or buttocks, or your legs become weak or numb — call 000 or go to ED immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New fever, unexplained weight loss, night pain, a history of cancer, or pain shooting down the leg past the knee with weakness
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests fracture/infection/malignancy or significant radiculopathy needing assessment.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange practical help to get off the floor safely and a means to obtain analgesia; low threshold for in-person review.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a back-muscle spasm — painful but not dangerous. Take regular pain relief and use a heat pack, and start gentle short walks tomorrow rather than staying in bed. A short muscle-relaxant course can help the worst of it. Let's arrange help to get you off the floor safely.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Analgesia + stay active + arrange transfer help/GP review; 000 for cauda equina features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000/ED immediately for bladder/bowel/saddle/leg-weakness features at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute non-specific mechanical low back pain; cauda equina and serious-cause red flags screened negative; analgesia + short muscle relaxant + stay active, no imaging; patient floor-bound/lives alone — needed transfer help.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring with no cauda equina screen/safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving cauda equina syndrome is missed and the patient is left with permanent bladder/bowel/leg impairment.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not arranging help for a floor-bound patient living alone / no serious-cause safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for benign mechanical back pain with a fully negative red-flag screen — usually unnecessary (though transfer help may be needed).
